text.div:
Generated Narrative: CodeSystem cs-nemsis-pertinent-negative

This case-sensitive code system https://fhirems.github.io/fhirEMSCoreIG/CodeSystem/cs-nemsis-pertinent-negative defines the following codes:

CodeDisplayDefinition
PN.Approximate ApproximateThe value is an estimate (e.g., estimated age).
PN.ContraindicationNoted Contraindication NotedA contraindication was identified that prevented the action.
PN.DeniedByOrder Denied By OrderThe action was denied by a physician or standing order.
PN.ExamFindingNotPresent Exam Finding Not PresentThe physical exam finding was specifically assessed and not found.
PN.MedicationAllergy Medication AllergyThe patient has a known allergy to the medication.
PN.MedicationAlreadyTaken Medication Already TakenThe patient had already taken the medication prior to EMS arrival.
PN.NoKnownDrugAllergy No Known Drug AllergyThe patient has no known drug allergies.
PN.NoneReported None ReportedNo pertinent history or findings were reported.
PN.NotImmunized Not ImmunizedThe patient has not received the relevant immunization.
PN.OrderCriteriaNotMet Order Criteria Not MetThe criteria required for a standing order were not met.
PN.Refused RefusedThe patient refused the assessment or intervention.
PN.SymptomNotPresent Symptom Not PresentThe symptom was specifically assessed and denied by the patient.
PN.UnableToComplete Unable To CompleteThe assessment or intervention could not be completed.
PN.Unresponsive UnresponsiveThe patient was unresponsive and unable to provide information.

name: NEMSISPertinentNegative
title: NEMSIS Pertinent Negative Codes
description: NEMSIS Pertinent Negative (PN) codes indicating a clinically significant documented absence. Applied to clinical data elements where documenting a negative finding is as meaningful as documenting a positive one.

concept.definition: The value is an estimate (e.g., estimated age).
A contraindication was identified that prevented the action.
The action was denied by a physician or standing order.
The physical exam finding was specifically assessed and not found.
The patient has a known allergy to the medication.
The patient had already taken the medication prior to EMS arrival.
The patient has no known drug allergies.
No pertinent history or findings were reported.
The patient has not received the relevant immunization.
The criteria required for a standing order were not met.
The patient refused the assessment or intervention.
The symptom was specifically assessed and denied by the patient.
The assessment or intervention could not be completed.
The patient was unresponsive and unable to provide information.
